21 May 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US Navy Lacks Capacity to Reopen Strait of Hormuz by Escorting Ships Sputnik 21 May 2026 -- The United States Navy lacks the capacity to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by providing escort services to ships through the contested waterway, US Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle said on Thursday.
  • US exhausts nearly half its THAAD stockpile shielding Israel from Iranian retaliation: Report Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The United States has shouldered the vast majority of Israel's missile defense during the war with Iran, firing far more advanced interceptors than the Israeli regime itself, according to Pentagon assessments.

  • Defying The US, Iran Is Cementing Its Control Over The Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- Shortly after the outbreak of the war with the United States and Israel, Iran took control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global oil and gas supplies.
  • May 21: Pezeshkian backs armed forces as IRGC ensures safe ship passage through Strait of Hormuz Press TV 21 May 2026 -- On Thursday, President Masoud Pezeshkian pledged the government's full support for Iran's armed forces as the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) reported the safe passage of 31 commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
  • IRGC Navy coordinates safe passage of 31 commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says 31 commercial vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, have safely passed through the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours under the coordination and protection of its Navy despite unprecedented insecurity caused by the "terrorist US military" in the Persian Gulf region.

  • Trump, Rubio Warn Against Iranian Tolling System In Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says that the United States does not want any tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz, as negotiations to end the war remain uncertain.
  • Trump says Iran talks "in final stages" as U.S. waits for "right answer" People's Daily 21 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that his administration is "in final stages of" peace talks with Iran and is willing to wait a few days for the "right answer."
  • Trumps Says War Could Escalate If Tehran Doesn't Have 'Right Answers' RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on May 20 that he is willing to wait "a few days" for Iran's response to Washington's latest proposal aimed at ending the war, but warned the situation could escalate quickly if Tehran fails to provide "the right answers."

  • Trump-Netanyahu turns tense, reveals US-Israel divide on Iran: Report Press TV 21 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump and Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu engaged in a heated conversation on Tuesday that exposed sharp differences over how to proceed with the war of aggression against Iran, according to a report.

  • Iran Restoring Its Military Forces Much Faster Than US Expected - Reports Sputnik 21 May 2026 -- Iran is restoring its armed forces and weapons production much faster than the United States expected, CNN reported on Thursday, citing sources familiar with US intelligence data.
  • IRGC Intelligence: US assumptions of 'easy and quick' operation against Iran proven false Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC)'s intelligence arm says that following the failed attacks in June and February and the collapse of an April siege, US assumptions about the feasibility of quick and easy military action against the Islamic Republic has proven false.
  • Pezeshkian commits government's full support to modernization of Iran's Armed Forces Press TV 21 May 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says the government will use "all available capacities" to strengthen the country's Armed Forces, praising the Iranian military's "decisive, intelligent, and deterrent" responses to the recent US-Israeli aggression.
  • Iran, not Washington, shaping regional corridor dynamics: Leader's aide Press TV 21 May 2026 -- A senior adviser on international affairs to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution has dismissed the United States' latest threats, stressing that regional corridor dynamics are shaped by Iran's realities on the ground, not by Washington's rhetoric.
  • Iran pursuing talks 'in good faith' but views US with 'deep suspicion': FM spox Press TV 21 May 2026 -- Iran is pursuing negotiations with the United States "in good faith" despite viewing Washington with "deep suspicion," the Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman said, confirming that multiple rounds of messages have been exchanged through Pakistani intermediaries based on Tehran's 14-point peace proposal.

  • Iraq Warns It Will Take Action Over Drone Attacks From Its Territory RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has warned of "necessary action" against those found responsible for launching attacks on neighboring Persian Gulf countries from Iraqi territory.

  • Nuclear issue can only be resolved by taking Iranian interests into account: Russia Press TV 21 May 2026 -- Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova says that the US-engineered crisis around Iran's nuclear issue can only be resolved through diplomatic channels and by taking Iranian interests into account, following the failed American-Israeli aggression.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War Department's 'Patriot Pipeline' Flows Talent to Arsenal of Freedom DoD News 21 May 2026 -- As the War Department rebuilds the defense industrial base — the thousands of private businesses that provide the hardware and weapons America's military uses to defend the nation — it will also ensure those businesses have the personnel to build those weapons.
  • Top Special Ops Civilian Recounts Successes, Lays Out Vision for Future DoD News 21 May 2026 -- Derrick Anderson, assistant secretary of war for special operations and low-intensity conflict, closed out the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention in Tampa, Florida, today with a keynote speech that focused on underscoring the SOF community's recent successes, as well as articulating his vision for its future.
  • Special Ops Conference Discusses Optimizing Human Performance DoD News 21 May 2026 -- Of the U.S. Special Operations Command's five "SOF truths," the first, "Humans are more important than hardware," was the most often repeated during the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention held this week in Tampa, Florida.
  • DAF leaders outline readiness, modernization priorities in FY27 budget testimony US Air Force 21 May 2026 -- Secretary of the Air Force Troy Meink, Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Ken Wilsbach and Chief of Space Operations Gen. Chance Saltzman told lawmakers, May 20, the Department of the Air Force's fiscal year 2027 budget request is designed to strengthen readiness, accelerate modernization and position both services to meet growing threats across the air and space domains.
  • Coast Guard awards contract for advanced training facilities to support future fleet readiness in Yorktown, Virginia US Coast Guard 21 May 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard has awarded a $17.8 million contract to Ocean Construction Services Inc. for the construction of new engineering and weapons training facilities at Training Center (TRACEN) Yorktown, Virginia.
  • MAKO Storm 2026 Prepares Navy Reserve Forces for High-End Maritime Operations US Navy 21 May 2026 -- Navy Warfare Development Center hosted the fifth annual MAKO Storm exercise May 14-17, bringing together U.S. Navy Reserve Sailors supporting U.S. Naval Forces Europe-Africa in addition to U.S. Sixth Fleet and U.S. Fifth Fleet for an advanced Maritime Operations Center training event focused on operational integration, decision-making, and fleet readiness.

  • Rubio Arrives In Sweden For NATO Meeting Amid US Troop Cuts RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has arrived in Sweden for a meeting of NATO foreign ministers as European allies seek reassurances over US troop drawdowns and Washington's shifting rhetoric on the alliance.
  • First RQ-4D Phoenix Operations in Norway mark NISRF Milestone NATO Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance Force (NISRF) 21 May 2026 -- On 21 May 2026, the NATO Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance Force (NISRF) achieved a significant milestone with the arrival of an RQ-4D Phoenix remotely piloted aircraft in Norway.
  • Finland hosts Agile Combat Employment-focused exercise, Imminent Field 26 NATO Allied Air Command 21 May 2026 -- Finland's Air Force is running its Imminent Field 26 highway-based exercise, previously known as the Baana exercise, from May 18 to 22, 2026, training dispersed flying operations alongside Italian F-35B fighters, enhancing Allied Air Command's (AIRCOM) Agile Combat Employment (ACE) readiness.

  • Andrii Sybiha held a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 21 May 2026
  • Andrii Sybiha Takes Part in Informal NATO-Ukraine Council Meeting at the Level of Foreign Ministers Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 21 May 2026
  • Ukraine - Hawk Missile System Sustainment US Dept. of State 21 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Ukraine for Hawk Missile System Sustainment and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $108.1 million.
  • Import procedures simplified for defense manufacturers: what changed Ukraine MOD 21 May 2026 -- Import procedures for foreign components have been simplified for Ukrainian weapons and military equipment manufacturers. The changes apply to the customs clearance stage.
  • Local communities will be able to directly fund air defense groups Ukraine MOD 21 May 2026 -- Ukraine is expanding a unique experimental project to strengthen air defense, allowing local self-government bodies to directly fund air defense groups within Volunteer Formations of Territorial Communities (VFTCs). At the same time, operational command of these groups will remain under the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine.
  • SSU and ESBU expose officials who embezzled over UAH 71 mln during food procurement for army in 2022-2024 Security Service of Ukraine 21 May 2026

  • Balikatan builds trust Australia DOD 21 May 2026 -- Australia stood shoulder to shoulder with the Philippines, the United States, New Zealand, Canada, Japan and France at Camp Aguinaldo, Manila, to close out the largest-ever Exercise Balikatan on May 8.
  • Boarding teams sharpen skills at sea Australia DOD 21 May 2026 -- Operating far from home in a complex maritime environment demands sharp skills, disciplined teamwork and absolute trust between partners.

  • Defence supports efforts to train Ukrainian military New Zealand Government 21 May 2026 -- The New Zealand Defence Force has joined a new multi-national initiative led by Norway to train Armed Forces of Ukraine personnel in Poland, Defence Minister Chris Penk says.
  • NZDF supports efforts in Eastern Europe to train Ukrainian military New Zealand Defence Force 21 May 2026 -- The New Zealand Defence Force (NZDF) has joined a new multi-national initiative led by Norway to train Armed Forces of Ukraine personnel in Poland.

  • U.S. Typhon missile system to be deployed again in Japan: media People's Daily 21 May 2026 -- The U.S. military will deploy its Typhon mid-range missile launcher and High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) to southwestern Japan next month for joint exercises with the Self-Defense Forces, local media reported, after the previous deployment drew strong local opposition.

  • The Department of National Defence Announces the Start of Construction at the Borden Transition Centre to Support Canadian Armed Forces Members Canada 21 May 2026 -- The Department of National Defence (DND) announces the start of construction for the new Transition Centre at Canadian Forces Base (CFB) Borden.
  • Department of National Defence to transition Nanisivik Naval Facility out of operational use Canada 21 May 2026 -- The Department of National Defence (DND) has begun the process of transitioning the Nanisivik Naval Facility site out of operational use after changes in operational needs and facility viability.

  • Israeli military will need up to 17,000 recruits to fill manpower gap: Officer Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The head of the Israeli military's personnel directorate's planning and personnel management division has raised the alarm about troop shortage in the regime's army, saying it will need up to 17,000 recruits to fill the manpower gap.

  • "The Turkish military is the most important assurance of regional and global peace" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 21 May 2026 -- Speaking at the Efes-2026 Military Exercise, President Erdoğan said: "The Turkish military is the military of peace. The Turkish military is the military of tranquility. The Turkish military is the military of stability. The Turkish military is the most important assurance of regional and global peace, tranquility and stability as well as of the security of its country and nation."
  • President Erdoğan observes Efes-2026 Military Exercise Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 21 May 2026 -- President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan attended the "Distinguished Observer Day" of the Efes-2026 Combined, Joint, and Live-Fire Field Exercise in the Seferihisar district of İzmir.

  • Eyes on the Ground: Joint Terminal Attack Controllers Guiding Air Power from the Front Lines Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 21 May 2026 -- There are just a couple dozen of them in the whole Czech military. No one is allowed to know what they look like. And yet they hold the key to one of the most expensive and lethal weapons on the modern battlefield — the fighter jet. This September, these elite service personnel from around the world will gather in the Czech Republic for Exercise Ample Strike 2026.
  • Minister Zůna's visit to Moldova: Defence Cooperation to Intensify Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 21 May 2026 -- Minister of Defence Jaromír Zůna visited Chișinău, where he met with his Moldovan counterpart, Minister Anatolie Nosatîi, during an official visit. They discussed options of further development of the cooperation between the two countries and current security matters. The Defence Ministers have honoured the memory of the fallen soldiers at the local Czechoslovak Legionaries Memorial.

  • Romania's participation in EFES 2026 Multinational Exercise Romania - Ministry of National Defence 21 May 2026 -- A Romanian Armed Forces' detachment participated in EFES 2026 Multinational Exercise conducted between April 20 and May 21st 2026, in the Republic of Turkey.

  • Polish fighter jets to defend Estonian airspace Estonia MFA 21 May 2026 -- Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur met today in Tallinn with Poland's Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of National Defence, Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz, to discuss deepening cooperation in defence and the defence industry, as well as the wider European security situation.

  • Government adopts plan on resilience of critical entities Finnish Government 21 May 2026 -- On 21 May 2026, the Government adopted a national plan on the resilience of critical entities (the CER strategy). The plan's objective is to enhance the resilience of critical entities. Cooperation between critical entities and authorities will enhance resilience and harmonise it at both EU and national level.

  • The Government asks hospitals and municipalities to update health emergency preparedness plans Government of Norway 21 May 2026 -- The Government is instructing the regional health authorities to review updated emergency preparedness plans in the health and care sector together with the municipalities. The plans must be strengthened in order to handle war and war-like situations. They must be based on a requirement to make 7,000 beds available for war casualties — both civilian and military.

  • Sweden's total defence in focus when Prime Minister received NATO Secretary General Government Offices of Sweden 21 May 2026 -- On 20-21 May, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson received NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. The meeting focused on Sweden's total defence, civil defence and resilience.

  • Meeting of Budrys with the Canadian Foreign Minister focuses on security, countering hybrid threats, and support for Ukraine Lithuania MFA 21 May 2026 -- On 20 May, the Minister of Foreign Affairs Kęstutis Budrys met with the Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand.
  • Meeting of Foreign Vice-Minister Plepytė with the Undersecretary of State of Poland focuses on strengthening NATO's eastern flank Lithuania MFA 21 May 2026 -- On 20 May in Vilnius, the Vice-Minister of Foreign Affairs Audra Plepytė met with the Undersecretary of State at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Poland, Robert Kupiecki.
  • Budrys in Tallinn: Current air defence solutions are not sufficient - it is necessary to move towards systemic solutions that tmatch the scale of today's threats Lithuania MFA 21 May 2026 -- On 20 May, the Minister of Foreign Affairs Kęstutis Budrys attended a meeting of the Ministers of Foreign Affairs of the three Baltic states and Canada, which focused on the region's security situation, support for Ukraine, and further strengthening transatlantic cooperation.
  • Gitanas Nausėda discussed the security of the Baltic airspace with the leaders of Latvia and Estonia President of the Republic of Lithuania 21 May 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda spoke by telephone on Thursday with Latvian President Edgars Rinkēvičs and Estonian President Alar Karis. The Baltic leaders discussed the recent increase in incidents in Baltic airspace, Russia's disinformation campaign against the Baltic States, and the need to strengthen NATO's eastern flank air defense and counter-drone capabilities.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with UK Foreign Secretary Cooper US Dept. of State 21 May 2026

  • 21 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 21 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 'Unjammable' Drones Pioneered In Ukraine Emerge In Middle East War RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- When an armed quadcopter buzzed over Lebanon's southern border into Israel on May 19, one Israel Defense Forces (IDF) soldier raced up a nearby hill and attempted to snag the drone's fiber-optic control cable with a piece of scrap metal.

  • Israel intercepts Gaza-bound aid flotilla, detains hundreds of activists People's Daily 21 May 2026 -- Israel said late Tuesday that it had intercepted all boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, detaining hundreds of activists and transporting them to Israel.

  • Hezbollah condemns US sanctions as desperate attempt to bolster Israeli aggression Press TV 21 May 2026 -- Hezbollah has strongly condemned the latest US sanctions imposed by the Departments of State and Treasury, describing them as a desperate bid by Washington to intimidate Lebanon's free people and revive the Israeli regime's failing aggression against the country.

  • Gaza reports over 125,000 cases of rat- and parasite-linked infections: UNRWA Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA) has reported more than 125,000 cases of infections linked to rats and parasites in the war-ravaged Gaza Strip during the first five months of the current year.
  • Gaza risks 'permanent' state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears UN News 21 May 2026 -- As Gaza's fragile ceasefire frays and humanitarian conditions deteriorate, a senior UN envoy warned the Security Council on Thursday that delays in implementing the Council-backed transition plan for the enclave will only increase suffering and undermine recovery.
  • Ceasefire, 'Far from Perfect', Remains Basis for Gaza Recovery, Speakers Tell Security Council amid Calls for Urgent, Full Implementation UN Security Council 21 May 2026 -- The tenuous ceasefire in Gaza is "far from perfect", with ongoing Israeli attacks and no tangible recovery in sight for territory's beleaguered Palestinian population, Gaza's High Representative on the newly established Board of Peace told the Security Council today.

  • U.S. Sanctioning Hizballah's Enablers in Lebanon US Dept. of State 21 May 2026 -- Today, the United States sanctioned nine individuals who enable Hizballah to undermine Lebanon's sovereignty.
  • Treasury Targets Hizballah-Aligned Officials Obstructing Peace and Disarmament US Treasury 21 May 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated nine individuals in Lebanon for obstructing the peace process in Lebanon and impeding the disarmament of Hizballah. These Hizballah-aligned officials include individuals embedded across Lebanon's parliament, military, and security sectors, where they seek to preserve the Iran-backed terrorist group's influence over key Lebanese state institutions. Hizballah's continued militant activity and coercive influence over the Lebanese state undermine the Lebanese government's ability to assert its authority over state institutions and disarm the terrorist group.
  • US pressures Palestinian UN envoy to withdraw UNGA candidacy, threatens visa revocations Press TV 21 May 2026 -- The administration of United States President Donald Trump has intensified efforts to pressure Palestinian Ambassador Riyad Mansour into withdrawing his candidacy for the vice-presidency of the UN General Assembly.

  • MFA Statement Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026 -- In light of the appalling circumstances of the detention and treatment of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, including Polish citizens, by the Israeli security services, with the consent and approval of Israel's Minister of National Security Itamar Ben-Gvir, Israel's chargé d'affaires a.i. in Warsaw has been summoned to the Polish MFA on 21 May 2026.
  • Israeli ambassadors summoned globally after hawkish minister threatens activists on Gaza flotilla Press TV 21 May 2026 -- Israeli ambassadors in several countries, including Italy, France, the Netherlands and Canada, have been summoned to hear strong protests over the degrading treatment of pro-Palestinian activists who were abducted from a flotilla bound for the besieged Gaza Strip.
  • Israel must take urgent steps to address the humanitarian situation in Gaza: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 21 May 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Palestine.
  • Israel: Statement by the Spokesperson European External Action Service (EEAS) 21 May 2026 -- The treatment of flotilla's activists in the video shared by Minister Ben Gvir is completely unacceptable, as also indicated by his own government colleagues.
  • Pakistan condemns illegal detention and mistreatment ofhumanitarian activists onboard Global Sumud Flotilla Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026 -- The Government of Pakistan condemns in the strongest possible terms the unlawful interception of the Global SumudFlotilla by Israeli occupying forces in the international waters as well as arbitrary detention and reported mistreatment of humanitarian workers onboard. Those detained also reportedly include Pakistani humanitarian activist Mr. Saad Edhi.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan UNOCHA 21 May 2026 -- Strike hits residential building killing family of 11

  • Secretary-General's message to the Protection of Civilians Week side event on "Upholding the Principle of Distinctions: Protection of Civilian Objects in Armed Conflict" UN Secretary-General 21 May 2026
  • 1 civilian killed every 14 minutes in armed conflicts worldwide in 2025: UN Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 21 May 2026 -- The UN warned Wednesday that civilians continue to pay a devastating price in conflicts around the world, with one civilian killed approximately every 14 minutes last year in armed conflicts monitored by the UN, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls UN News 21 May 2026

  • PH right to improve WPS islands non-negotiable Philippine News Agency 21 May 2026 -- Sen. Jinggoy Estrada on Thursday said the country's right to develop and improve islands in the Kalayaan Island Group is "legitimate, lawful, and non-negotiable," rejecting China's renewed assertion of "indisputable sovereignty" over the West Philippine Sea.

  • Côte d'Ivoire reaffirmed its firm and constant position regarding the territorial integrity and sovereignty of the Kingdom over its entire territory, including the Sahara region. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026

  • Reds aware of dwindling forces - NTF-ELCAC Philippine News Agency 21 May 2026 -- Even the remaining communist insurgents are well aware of their dwindling offensive capabilities, as well as their capacity to influence communities, a ranking National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) said on Thursday.

  • Foreign Ministry: Saudi Arabia Condemns Cowardly Terrorist Attack on Military Base in Chad SPA 21 May 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation and denunciation of the cowardly terrorist attack on a military base in the Republic of Chad, which resulted in the deaths and injuries of several security personnel, reiterating the Kingdom's firm rejection of terrorist and extremist acts that attempt to undermine the security and stability of Chad and its brotherly people

  • Haitian children 'paying the highest price' amid surge in gang recruitment UN News 21 May 2026 -- As gangs continue to "terrorise" communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.

  • ICC concludes confirmation of charges hearing in El Hishri case ICC 21 May 2026 -- Today, 21 May 2026, the confirmation of charges hearing in the case The Prosecutor v. Khaled Mohamed Ali El Hishri concluded before Pre-Trial Chamber I of the International Criminal Court (ICC), composed of Judge Iulia Antoanella Motoc (Presiding Judge), and Judges Reine Adélaïde Sophie Alapini-Gansou and María del Socorro Flores Liera.

News Reports

  • UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo UN News 21 May 2026 -- United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support.
  • UK steps up support to stop spread of Ebola in eastern DRC UK FCDO 21 May 2026 -- The UK has announced up to £20 million to support communities affected by the deadly Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs delivers note to the Russian embassy regarding disinformation campaign against Estonia Estonia MFA 21 May 2026 -- Today, 21 May, the Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the Chargé d'Affaires ad interim of the Russian Federation to deliver a note concerning the ongoing disinformation campaign directed against Estonia and the other Baltic States.
  • The Polish Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses its condemnation of Russian threats and disinformation aimed at Latvia and other states Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026 -- Polish MFA expresses solidarity and unequivocal support for Latvia and other countries facing increasing pressure from Russia. We condemn false narratives and unjustified claims spread by Russia regarding the alleged use of the territory or airspace of Latvia or that of other NATO Allies by Ukrainian drones to carry out attacks against Russia.

  • Nuclear Triad Should Serve as Guarantor of Sovereignty of Union State - Putin Sputnik 21 May 2026 -- Given rising tensions globally and new threats and risks, the nuclear triad must serve as a guarantor of the sovereignty of the Union State of Russia and Belarus, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday.
  • Russian Aviation Units Practice Equipping Kinzhal Missiles With Special Warheads Sputnik 21 May 2026 -- Aviation units of the Russian aerospace forces, as part of the nuclear forces exercise, are practicing equipping Kinzhal missiles with special warheads and flying into patrol areas, the Russian Defense Ministry said on Thursday.
  • Iskander-M Missiles With Special Warheads Deployed in Belarus Drills Sputnik 21 May 2026 -- As part of nuclear forces exercises in Belarus, Iskander-M operational-tactical missile systems equipped with special warheads have been deployed, the Russian Defense Ministry said in a statement.
  • UK condemns Russia's destabilising nuclear rhetoric and RS-28 Sarmat missile signalling: UK Statement to the OSCE UK FCDO 21 May 2026 -- UK Senior Military Advisor, Colonel Joby Rimmer condemns Russia's sustained use of nuclear rhetoric and recent Sarmat missile testing. This pattern of coercive signalling heightens instability, erodes trust, and increases the risk of miscalculation, undermining established security commitments and transparency.

  • Fading western powers using sanctions, fear to halt rise of multipolar world: Russian FM Press TV 21 May 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov said that Western powers are resorting to sanctions, economic coercion, and political interference as their global dominance wanes and emerging powers reshape the international order.

  • President Xi Jinping Has a Tea Chat with Russian President Vladimir Putin PRC MofA 21 May 2026 -- On the evening of May 20, President Xi Jinping had a tea chat with Russian President Vladimir Putin at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Xi, Putin hail "new stage" of ties in Beijing meeting People's Daily 21 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday hailed a "new stage" in bilateral ties as they agreed to further extend a China-Russia friendship treaty and pledged to work together for a more just and equitable world order.
  • Xi, Putin hail "new stage" of ties in Beijing meeting PRC MND 21 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday hailed a "new stage" in bilateral ties as they agreed to further extend a China-Russia friendship treaty and pledged to work together for a more just and equitable world order.
  • Xi holds talks with Putin in Beijing PRC MND 21 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping held talks with visiting Russian President Vladimir Putin at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Wednesday, with the two sides agreeing to further extend the China-Russia Treaty of Good-Neighborliness and Friendly Cooperation.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 21, 2026 PRC MofA 21 May 2026
  • National cyber defense agencies warn against new 'Silver Fox' Trojan variant targeting Chinese users Global Times 21 May 2026 -- China's National Computer Virus Emergency Response Center warned on Thursday that a new "Silver Fox" Trojan variant has been identified, specially targeting Chinese users. The malware spread through files disguised with labels such as "internal investigation results," "disciplinary violation list" and "layoff compensation." Once a computer is infected, it could be remotely controlled, its data stolen, and potentially exploited by cybercriminals in telecom and online fraud schemes, according to the warning.

  • Taiwan rejects Trump's claim it 'stole' U.S. chip industry CNA 21 May 2026 -- The Cabinet on Thursday said that Taiwan's semiconductor industry is "the result of long and painstaking independent development by local companies," rejecting claims of theft by U.S. President Donald Trump.
  • INTERVIEW / Top U.S. envoy affirms Taiwan commitments despite Trump's remarks CNA 21 May 2026 -- The United States' top envoy to Taiwan has reaffirmed that Washington's long-standing commitments to Taiwan have not changed after U.S. President Donald Trump called arms sales to Taiwan a "good bargaining chip" in dealings with China.
  • Foreign media reports China signaled it holds up Pentagon official's visit until Trump decides on Taiwan island arms sale, Chinese FM responds Global Times 21 May 2026 -- China's opposition to US arms sales to China's Taiwan region is consistent and clear, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Thursday, in response to a question that the Financial Times cited sources as saying that China is holding up a proposed visit by the Pentagon's under-secretary of defense for policy, as China signals that it couldn't approve the visit before US President Donald Trump makes a decision on an arms sales package to Taiwan region.
  • China firmly opposes US official exchanges with Taiwan region, arms sales to island, says FM over Trump claiming he will talk to Lai Global Times 21 May 2026 -- China firmly opposes official exchanges between the US and China's Taiwan region, and firmly opposes US arms sales to Taiwan region. This position is consistent, clear and firm, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Thursday in response to a media inquiry that US President Donald Trump claimed on Wednesday that he will talk to Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te about a possible arms sale while Lai said on Thursday that he would be happy to speak with Trump.
  • Trump revives tensions with China, declares intent to talk to Taiwan leader Press TV 21 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has again signaled his intention to speak directly with the leader of Chinese Taipei (Taiwan), reviving a provocative break from decades-old diplomatic norms and risking fresh tensions with China over the Taiwan issue.
  • Taiwan targets 100,000 monthly drone output, export growth CNA 21 May 2026 -- Taiwan's drone industry is expected to expand significantly by 2030, with projected monthly production capacity of 100,000 units and 50 percent exported, the Ministry of Economic Affairs (MOEA) said Thursday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 21 May 2026

  • U.S. and ROK's Reckless Arms Build-up Will Incite DPRK to Further Strength Military Deterrent: KCNA Commentary KCNA 21 May 2026 -- The U.S. has further boosted its arms sales to its vassal countries in the Korean peninsula and the Northeast Asian region, and this has emerged as a challenge to regional security that should no longer be overlooked.

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (May 21, 2026) ROK MFA 21 May 2026

  • Prime Minister's Office Conversion Authority Holds National Convert Conference Under the Banner "An Eternal Nation - One Nation" Israel - Prime Minister 21 May 2026

  • Trump says there is no reason for US to escalate further in Cuba: 'The place is falling apart' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 21 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said Wednesday there is no reason for his administration to escalate efforts on Cuba following months of increasing pressure on the island nation, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • China firmly supports Cuba in safeguarding national sovereignty, dignity: Chinese FM on Cuba's rejection of US accusations against Raul Castro Global Times 21 May 2026 -- Asked about China's comment on the Cuban government's rejection of recent accusations made by the US against Cuban leader Raul Castro, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Thursday that China consistently and firmly opposes unilateral sanctions that have no basis in international law or authorization of the UN Security Council.
  • Russian Foreign Ministry statement regarding a new round of tensions between Cuba and the United States Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026
  • Termination of Green Card of Cuban National Tied to Communist Regime's Top Financial Official US Dept. of State 21 May 2026 -- Today, Adys Lastres Morera, a Cuban national with ties to the communist regime in Havana, was arrested following the Department of State's termination of her lawful permanent resident (LPR) status, at my direction.

  • Attacked By The State And Fractured Within, Belarus Opposition Struggles In Exile RFE/RL 21 May 2026 -- Earlier this month, a prominent member of the Belarusian opposition in exile laid out an "ideal scenario" for online elections to the Coordination Council, a shadow parliament formed by foes of authoritarian leader Aleksandr Lukashenko: Imagine if 1 million people voted.
  • Political prisoners in Belarus: Joint Statement to the OSCE Permanent Council, May 2026 UK FCDO 21 May 2026 -- The UK and 37 other countries mark the International Day of Solidarity with Political Prisoners in Belarus, calling for immediate and unconditional prisoner releases.

  • Joint Press Release of the 5th Meeting of India-Egypt Joint Working Group on Counter Terrorism India MEA 21 May 2026 -- The 5th meeting of the Joint Working Group (JWG) on Counter Terrorism between India and Egypt was held in New Delhi on May 20, 2026.

  • 8th India-France Maritime Cooperation Dialogue France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 21 May 2026

  • Security is teamwork: Foreign Minister Wadephul is travelling to a meeting of NATO Foreign Ministers in Helsingborg and to the GLOBSEC Forum in Prague Germany Federal Foreign Office 21 May 2026 -- On 21 and 22 May, the NATO Ministers of Foreign Affairs will be meeting in Helsingborg, Sweden. At their meeting, Ministers will prepare for the upcoming NATO Summit in Ankara and also discuss how to further strengthen and enhance the effectiveness of the North Atlantic Alliance.
  • Foreign Office News Newsroom Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Helsingborg and Prague Germany Federal Foreign Office 21 May 2026 -- Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Helsingborg and Prague

  • Minister Anand meets with Minister of Foreign Affairs of Estonia in Tallinn Canada 21 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Margus Tsahkna, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Estonia, on the margins of the Canada-Baltics "3+1" Foreign Ministers meeting taking place in Tallinn, Estonia.
  • Minister Anand meets with Minister of Foreign Affairs of Lithuania Canada 21 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, yesterday met with Kęstutis Budrys, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Lithuania, on the margins of the Canada-Baltics "3+1" Foreign Ministers meeting in Tallinn.
  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with Chancellor of Germany Friedrich Merz and Prime Minister of Norway Jonas Gahr Støre Prime Minister of Canada 21 May 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the Chancellor of Germany, Friedrich Merz, and the Prime Minister of Norway, Jonas Gahr Støre.

  • Kazakhstan targets 30% nuclear production localization before first NPP construction Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 21 May 2026 -- Kazakhstan has adopted the Comprehensive Plan for the Development of Production Localization for the Nuclear Industry for 2026-2030, Qazinform News Agency reported.

  • Secretary General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Jonatan Vseviov visited Norway Estonia MFA 21 May 2026 -- Secretary General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Jonatan Vseviov visited Norway on 20-21 May

  • Risch, Colleagues Statement on Violent Protests in Bolivia US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 21 May 2026 -- U.S. Senators Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio), John Curtis (R-Utah), and Rick Scott (R-Fla.) today released the statement below on the ongoing violent attempts to destabilize Bolivia.

  • Spanish Government expresses solidarity with Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania in the face of Russian disinformation campaign and drone attacks Spain MFA 21 May 2026

  • Designation of Tanzanian Police Force Official for Involvement in Gross Violations of Human Rights US Dept. of State 21 May 2026 -- The Department of State is designating Tanzanian Police Force (TPF) Senior Assistant Commissioner Faustine Jackson Mafwele under Section 7031(c) based on credible information that he was involved in gross violations of human rights.
